articles.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
articles.description: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

articles.title: Tech stocks plunge in Asia after record rally and renewed Middle East attacks
articles.description: Markets in South Korea and Japan slid after a rally in tech stocks in recent weeks.

articles.title: Copper Rebounds From Friday Slump on China-Buying and US Flows
articles.description: Copper edged up to recoup some of last week’s losses as buying activity in China and flows of metal to the US supported the demand outlook.

articles.title: Israel says it has struck Iran after taking missile fire
articles.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Israel launched airstrikes early Monday targeting central and western Iran in response to missile fire from Tehran, attacks that threatened to drag the wider Middle East back into a regional war. articles.title: Korea’s AI Impact Sparks Pressure Across Government Bond Market
articles.description: Investor fervor for artificial intelligence that has driven South Korea’s stock market to the top of global rankings is taking a toll on another market: government bonds.

articles.title: How Mexican cartels turned South African farms into meth production hubs
articles.description: Raids on South African farms have uncovered meth labs linked to Mexican networks, signalling a new cartel phase.

articles.title: Oppenheimer: Trump holds the reins on Netanyahu’s escalation options
articles.description: Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu has incentives to escalate tensions with Iran but is constrained by US President Trump.

articles.title: Psychiatrist Robert Coles, Pulitzer Prize-winning author who championed needs of children, dies
articles.description: Harvard University professor Robert Coles, the psychiatrist and Pulitzer Prize-winning author who championed the cause of children grappling with poverty and segregation, has died at 97, his son said Sunday. articles.title: A 7.8 magnitude earthquake rocks the southern Philippines, causing some damage and 1-meter tsunami
articles.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — A magnitude 7.8 earthquake centered at sea shook part of the southern Philippines early Monday, causing damage in a key coastal city, knocking down power and setting off 1-meter (3-foot) tsunami waves along nearby coasts, officials said.
